header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Strategic Program Manager who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve enablement and adoption—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Design {coord|enablement} plans and documentation across 20+ programs, building guides that cut onboarding time by 75%
Deliver customized live {prog|training sessions} and webinars for 600+ participants, driving {ex|adoption} and a 78% program enrollment rate
Conduct needs assessments with 25 global {cross|stakeholders} to inform engagement tactics, designing 3 new programs best fit to their goals
Build relationships across {cross|Marketing, Tech, and Sales}, analyzing {data|metrics} and feedback to identify champions and expansion opportunities
Upskill the client team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via evidence-based insights
Empower stakeholders to present executive-ready insights on $362K in costs, leading to redesigned policies and training
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ {ex|customer/employee experiences} to amplify voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved {ex|experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Optimized onboarding processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Piloted an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
Directed a Merger & Acquisition project, migrating and merging employee records across systems
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants

changeLog: 2026-05-30: Tailored for Figma — Customer Enablement Manager (SF/NY/remote → Portland header, remote-eligible). Summary → Strategic Program Manager, enablement/adoption flavor. InGenius → Set A: enablement plans + documentation, customized training delivery + 78% enrollment, needs assessments to inform engagement tactics, relationship-building + metrics analysis + champion/expansion identification (direct JD language). Reordered to lead with enablement-plan bullet. ODN → Pair A (quantified). NextGen → 'customer/employee experiences'. Skills → kept LMS, dropped HRCI. NOTE: JD wants 4+ yrs TAM/CS/Enablement in SaaS — Jamie's enablement is program/internal; truthful overlap on adoption strategy, enablement plan design, training delivery, stakeholder mgmt.
